RevisionEvent.Note: Only known prior ReleaseDate of the metadata
Added HelioData InformationURL, Updated to SPASE Version 2.7.1. JWL

ResourceHeader.Description: The Maputo Observatory, historically known as the Campos Rodrigues Observatory, was founded in 1908 in Lourenço Marques (now Maputo), Mozambique. Originally built during the Portuguese overseas empire, it was a key astronomical, meteorological, and seismological station for the region. Today, Maputo is also home to the Observatory of Cultural Policies in Africa (OCPA).
ResourceHeader.Acknowledgement: Departamento de Geofisica Global,Direccao, National de Geologia, Maputo,Mozambique

InformationURL.Name: HelioData
InformationURL.Description: The HelioData mission page provides an overview of the mission and more, such as all SPASE dataset records associated with it.
